title: Store Operations Report
abstract: This bundled sample shows the minimum depth expected for an analytical PDF: scope, evidence, drivers, risks, and actions.

blocks.content: Revenue reached HKD 91,838 in the reporting window, with repeat customers contributing a disproportionate share of growth.
AOV held at HKD 342, which suggests topline expansion came more from order frequency and retention than from ticket-size inflation.
The main operating risk is revenue concentration in a small VIP segment; retention remains strong, but concentration raises downside sensitivity.

rows: Growth outpaced prior period and was not purely price-led.

blocks.title: Finding 1
blocks.content: Repeat customers contributed more than half of revenue, indicating that current growth is being sustained by retention rather than a broad acquisition surge.

blocks.title: Finding 2
blocks.content: AOV remained nearly flat, which means conversion quality and purchase cadence matter more than pricing for the next optimization cycle.

blocks.title: Finding 3
blocks.content: VIP concentration rose, which improves short-term productivity but raises downside risk if the top cohort becomes less active.
Protect the repeat-purchase engine first: reactivation and loyalty motions should be prioritized because retention is the primary growth driver in the current window.
Reduce VIP concentration risk by expanding the mid-tier cohort with targeted cross-sell and bundle offers.
Validate whether recent gains came from campaign timing, product mix, or customer behavior before scaling spend.
